source_artifact: KB node perspective/subtraction_design (VALIDATED_MECHANISM_2026_07_05 and related fields), checked 2026-07-06

claims.statement: The 4-phase mechanism computes correct sign and magnitude for a-b over positive integers
claims.reproducer: worked_example/subtraction_check.py — sweep a,b in 1..255 both signs, 0 failures (wider than node's 1..127)
claims.notes: Independently re-implemented from node text only (cold-read). Encoding risk: 'overhang as rightbit - borrow' was the least explicit phrase in the node; implemented literally and the sweep confirms that reading.

claims.statement: The frontier table's 6 entries are correct: 5 uniquely forced by ground truth, (1,1,1) borrow_out is a don't-care and the node's (0,0) is valid
claims.reproducer: worked_example/subtraction_table_derivation.py — per key, brute-force all (out_bit,borrow_out) yielding correct magnitude, intersect across all cases to width 9
claims.notes: Derived FROM ground truth, not checked against the node's table — the table could not have smuggled an error through. Don't-care arises because same-length cases have empty overhang, discarding borrow_out.

claims.statement: Frontier keys (1,1,0) and (0,1,0) are unreachable: b1=1 implies b2=1

derived_from.rule: chain: b1=1 -> nonzero low result bit exists (C3b) -> phase-2 borrow fires and sticks (C3a) -> b2=1

claims.reproducer: worked_example/subtraction_check.py C3 section — sweep to width 10 shows (b1,b2)=(1,0) never occurs; proof upgrades sweep to all widths
claims.notes: This was the node's open item 4 (asserted, not proven). Now closed.

claims.statement: In phase 2 (0 - bit - rb), b2=0 iff all phase-1 low result bits are 0: 0-0-0 never borrows, the first 1-bit forces a borrow, and once rb=1 it holds (0-bit-1 < 0 always)
claims.reproducer: inspection of the phase-2 recurrence (three one-line cases); mechanically corroborated by the width-10 sweep in subtraction_check.py
claims.notes: 'given' = read directly off the recurrence, not derived from other table rows.

claims.statement: If phase 1 generates any borrow, the FIRST borrow-generating position has borrow_in=0, hence (a_i,b_i)=(0,1), diff=-1, result bit 1 — so b1=1 implies a nonzero low result bit
claims.reproducer: inspection of the phase-1 recurrence at the first borrow position; corroborated by the same sweep
claims.notes: Falsification check: could a LATER position zero that bit out? No — phase-1 result bits are written once, LSB->MSB, never revisited.

claims.statement: Trigger 'negative iff right_multibit OR (right_here=1 AND b1=1)' is equivalent to a<b
claims.reproducer: worked_example/subtraction_check.py — the C1 sweep routes every case through this trigger; any inequivalence would surface as a sign failure
claims.notes: Verified only jointly with C1, not in isolation. A closed-form argument (left MSB always 1) looks available but was not written; evidence_class deliberately NOT upgraded.

claims.statement: Exactly one of the six same-length frontier combos goes negative: left=1, right=1, borrow_in=1

derived_from.rule: restriction of the trigger to the same-length frontier enumeration (right in {0,1,gone} x borrow in {0,1})

claims.reproducer: worked_example/subtraction_check.py — same joint sweep as C4
claims.notes: Inherits C4's evidence class; upgrading C4 to proof would upgrade this too.
